activities_projects.title: Identifying Factors of Production
activities_projects.objective: Understand the four requirements for production of goods and services
steps: Visit a local shop, farm, or small manufacturing unit
List the Land, Labour, Physical Capital (Fixed and Working), and Human Capital involved
Categorize tools and machines versus raw materials and cash
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Distinguishes between fixed capital and working capital
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct categorization of the four factors (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mapping Land Use
activities_projects.objective: Understand how land is distributed and used in a village setting
steps: Draw a hypothetical map of Palampur based on the chapter description
Shade areas for large farmers, small farmers, and landless laborers
Mark common resources like the village pond or grazing land
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes the inequality in land distribution
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of map according to chapter data (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Green Revolution Comparison
activities_projects.objective: Compare traditional farming with modern farming methods
steps: Create a T-chart comparing 'Traditional Seeds' vs 'HYV Seeds'
List the inputs required for each (Water, Chemical fertilizers, Pesticides)
Note the difference in yield/output based on the text
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes the impact of the Green Revolution on productivity
activities_projects.evaluation: Detailed comparison of inputs and outputs (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Multiple Cropping Simulation
activities_projects.objective: Learn how farmers grow more than one crop on the same piece of land
steps: Divide a year into Kharif, Rabi, and Zaid seasons
Assign specific crops (e.g., Jowar/Bajra, Wheat, Potato) to each season
Explain how irrigation makes this possible throughout the year
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understand the concept and benefits of multiple cropping
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct seasonal placement of crops (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Labour and Wages Survey
activities_projects.objective: Understand the conditions of farm laborers and minimum wages
steps: Role-play an interview between a landless laborer (like Dala) and a large farmer
Discuss why wages might be lower than the government-fixed minimum wage
List factors like competition for work and debt
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies challenges faced by unorganized sector workers
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of reasoning in the role-play/interview notes (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Capital in Farming: The Interest Trap
activities_projects.objective: Understand how small farmers arrange capital compared to large farmers
steps: Calculate interest on a loan of ₹5,000 at 24% interest for 4 months (Money lender rate)
Compare it with a bank loan rate of 8% per annum
Write a short paragraph on why Savita (small farmer) struggles to save
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects lack of savings to the cycle of debt
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct mathematical calculation and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Market Surplus Case Study
activities_projects.objective: Learn what happens to the wheat produced by large farmers
steps: Trace the journey of surplus wheat from Tejpal Singh's farm to the market at Raiganj
Explain how the earnings from the surplus are used (Buying tractors, lending money)
Contrast this with a small farmer who has no surplus
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical flow of the surplus distribution process (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Non-Farm Activities Gallery
activities_projects.objective: Identify economic activities other than farming in a village
steps: Identify the four non-farm activities mentioned in Palampur (Dairy, Small-scale manufacturing, Trade, Transport)
Describe the 'fixed capital' and 'labour' used in one of these activities
Note how these activities provide employment
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the diversity of rural economy
activities_projects.evaluation: Clear description of non-farm sectors (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Modern vs. Traditional Irrigation
activities_projects.objective: Observe the transition from Persian wheels to electric tubewells
steps: Draw or describe a Persian wheel and an electric tubewell
List three advantages of electric-run tubewells for a farmer
Explain how electricity changed the life of people in Palampur

activities_projects.title: Environmental Impact Report
activities_projects.objective: Critically analyze the sustainability of modern farming
steps: Research or read about the depletion of groundwater due to tubewells
List the effects of chemical fertilizers on soil fertility
Suggest two ways to make farming in Palampur sustainable
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the environmental cost of the Green Revolution
activities_projects.evaluation: Critical thinking and quality of suggestions (10 marks)
